overview.title: multichain-wallet-demo
overview.description: This demo focuses on the engineering abstraction of multichain wallet integration. The goal is not just connecting wallets, but separating connection, network detection, signature adapters, transaction adapters, wallet binding and error normalization into a maintainable frontend flow.
overview.sessionDescription: The page only consumes normalized account, chain, wallet and error state.
overview.point1Description: EVM, Solana, BTC and Sei all have different account, signature and transaction models.
overview.point2Title: The page does not handle chain logic directly
overview.point2Description: The page consumes a unified WalletAdapter capability surface instead of branching on SDK specifics.
overview.point3Title: Connection state and business binding state are separate
overview.point3Description: A successful wallet connection does not mean the business account binding is complete. These states should be modeled independently.
overview.point4Description: Different wallet SDK errors should be normalized into MultiChainError to avoid scattered UI branches.

walletControl.title: Wallet Connect
walletControl.description: Select an ecosystem, choose a wallet, then connect through the unified adapter layer.
walletControl.tonReserved: TON is reserved in this demo. No real wallet integration is wired yet.
walletControl.pickSolanaFirst: Pick a Solana wallet first, then connect it.
walletControl.noSeiWalletDetected: No Sei wallet detected. Install Compass, Keplr, or Leap.
walletControl.networkStatusDescription: Normalized network state from the active adapter. Useful for demos that require switching chains before signing or transacting.
walletControl.manualSwitchHint: This wallet does not expose a programmatic network switch. Change the network manually in the wallet UI.
walletControl.nonEvmSwitchHint: {ecosystem} usually does not follow the EVM-style switchChain model. Network context is usually controlled by the wallet itself or by the provider injected into the page.
walletControl.errorNoAccountReturned: Wallet connected but no account was returned.

intentLab.title: Signature & Transaction Lab
intentLab.description: Test wallet capabilities through one unified adapter surface: message signing, typed-data signing and demo transaction dispatch.
intentLab.signatureDescription: EVM supports personal_sign and EIP-712. Solana, BTC and Sei usually use ecosystem-specific message signing.
intentLab.transactionDescription: EVM can send a native transfer. BTC is modeled as PSBT. Solana and Sei are represented as program or chain-specific calls.

sendIntent.errorUnsupportedTransaction: Transaction is not supported by current adapter
